name: pycifrw

metadata.name: PyCifRW
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Python Software Foundation License
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
PyCIFRW Readme
--------------

Introduction
------------

PyCIFRW provides support for reading and writing CIF (Crystallographic
Information Format) files using Python.  It was developed at the
Australian National Beamline Facility (ANBF), run by the Australian
Synchrotron Research Program (ASRP), as part of a larger project to
provide CIF input/output capabilities for data collection.  It is
now (Nov 2022) maintained and developed within the Australian Nuclear Science and
Technology Organisation (ANSTO).

Conformance
-----------

The specifications found in Vol G of the International Tables for
Crystallography were used as a reference for CIF 1.0/1.1 syntax.
http://dx.doi.org/10.1107/S1600576715021871 was used as the CIF 2.0
reference.

PyCifRW has been tested on the IUCr sample CIF1.1 trip files located at
http://www.iucr.org/iucr-top/cif/developers/trip and fails or
successfully reads as it is supposed to (note that `ciftest5` contains
characters now forbidden in CIFs).

Supported Platforms
-------------------

PyCIFRW is written entirely in Python, and so should run wherever
Python runs.  Any failures on Mac, Windows or Linux should be
communicated to the author, either through the Github issues
tracker (http://github.com/jamesrhester/pycifrw/issues) or email.

The source code of a C extension module is also included in the
distribution.  This module accelerates CIF file reading. From time to
time system-dependent installation packages are generated containing
precompiled versions of this module.

Installation
------------

See file [INSTALLATION](https://github.com/jamesrhester/pycifrw/blob/development/INSTALLATION).

License
----------

PyCIFRW is made available using the Python 2.0 license.  The full text is [here](https://github.com/jamesrhester/pycifrw/blob/development/LICENSE)

Use
---

See the various files in the docs directory for details of the interface.
Essentially, CIF files look like python dictionaries, with each
entry in the dictionary corresponding to a data block.  The blocks
themselves are also dictionaries, with each data name being a
single entry in the dictionary, so for example,
`cf['si_std']['_diffrn_meas_wavelength']` will return the value of
`_diffrn_meas_wavelength` in the data block named `si_std` of the Cif file object
`cf`.

Example
-------

To read in a CIF:

    from CifFile import ReadCif
    cf = ReadCif('jun_01_2.cif')

to access information in a CIF

    wav = cf['si_std']['_diffrn_meas_wavelength']

to set a value

    cf['si_std']['_diffrn_meas_wavelength'] = 1.54


Example programs
----------------

The file 'TestPyCIFRW.py' in the source distribution contains around 170 tests
of PyCIFRW functionality and is a good source of examples for using both simple
and advanced PyCIFRW interfaces.

The "Programs" directory in the source distribution contains simple example programs.
Of interest are `validate_cif.py` which validates a data files against data dictionaries
(execute this file at a terminal prompt without arguments for a help message)
and `output_asciidoc.py` which will convert a DDLm dictionary into an
asciidoc document that can then be converted to HTML or other presentation
formats.

modules.name: YappsStarParser_STAR2
documentation: To maximize python3/python2 compatibility

functions.name: YappsStarParser_STAR2.stripextras
documentation: Strip extras gets rid of leading and trailing whitespace, and
semicolons.

functions.name: YappsStarParser_STAR2.stripstring
documentation: helper function to get rid of inverted commas etc.

functions.name: YappsStarParser_STAR2.striptriple
documentation: helper function to get rid of triple quotes

functions.name: YappsStarParser_STAR2.makeloop
documentation: helper function to populate a StarBlock given a list of names
and values .
Note that there may be an empty list at the very end of our itemlists,
so we remove that if necessary.

functions.name: YappsStarParser_STAR2.make_empty
documentation: return an object with the appropriate amount of nesting

functions.name: YappsStarParser_STAR2.cif_update
documentation: this function updates a dictionary first checking for name collisions,
which imply that the CIF is invalid.  We need case insensitivity for
names.
Unfortunately we cannot check loop item contents against non-loop contents
in a non-messy way during parsing, as we may not have easy access to previous
key value pairs in the context of our call (unlike our built-in access to all
previous loops).
For this reason, we don't waste time checking looped items against non-looped
names during parsing of a data block.  This would only match a subset of the
final items.   We do check against ordinary items, however.
Note the following situations:
(1) new_dict is empty -> we have just added a loop; do no checking
(2) new_dict is not empty -> we have some new key-value pairs

functions.name: YappsStarParser_STAR2.order_update
documentation: this takes two lines, so we couldn't fit it into a one line execution statement...

functions.name: YappsStarParser_STAR2.pairwise
documentation: and finally...turn a sequence into a python dict (thanks to Stackoverflow)

Failed to index ``Programs/output_asciidoc.py`` due to ``Syntax Error @ 179:17.
parser error: error at 178:43: expected one of !=, %, &, (, *, **, +, ,, -, ., /, //, :, ;, <, <<, <=, =, ==, >, >=, >>, @, NEWLINE, [, ^, and, if, in, is, not, or, |

    return False
                ^``

modules.name: Programs.star2_to_cif2
documentation:
Convert a data/dictionary file in STAR2 to CIF2 format.  STAR2 and CIF2 differ
most markedly in their compound data value delimiters (comma and whitespace
respectively).

We read in the file using STAR2 grammar, then output with CIF2 grammar.

Two optional files may be provided, one giving a top-level comment, and
another the descriptive text for the dictionary.

This version of the Yapps 2 Runtime can be distributed under the
terms of the MIT open source license, either found in the LICENSE file
included with the Yapps distribution
<http://theory.stanford.edu/~amitp/yapps/> or at
<http://www.opensource.org/licenses/mit-license.php>
Modified for PyCIFRW by JRH to allow external scanner
To maximize python3/python2 compatibility

functions.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.print_line_with_pointer
documentation:
Print the line of 'text' that includes position 'p',
along with a second line with a single caret (^) at position p
Note that this sort of error printout is useless with the
compiled scanner

functions.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.print_error
documentation: Print error messages, the parser stack, and the input text -- for human-readable error messages.

classes.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.YappsSyntaxError
documentation: When we run into an unexpected token, this is the exception to use

classes.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.NoMoreTokens
documentation: Another exception object, for when we run out of tokens

classes.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Scanner

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Scanner.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the scanner.

Parameters:
  patterns : [(terminal, uncompiled regex), ...] or None
  ignore : [terminal,...]
  input : string

If patterns is None, we assume that the subclass has
defined self.patterns : [(terminal, compiled regex), ...].
Note that the patterns parameter expects uncompiled regexes,
whereas the self.patterns field expects compiled regexes.

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Scanner.get_token_pos
documentation: Get the current token position in the input text.

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Scanner.get_char_pos
documentation: Get the current char position in the input text.

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Scanner.get_prev_char_pos
documentation: Get the previous position (one token back) in the input text.

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Scanner.get_line_number
documentation: Get the line number of the current position in the input text.

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Scanner.get_column_number
documentation: Get the column number of the current position in the input text.

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Scanner.get_input_scanned
documentation: Get the portion of the input that has been tokenized.

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Scanner.get_input_unscanned
documentation: Get the portion of the input that has not yet been tokenized.

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Scanner.interp_token
documentation:
Get the i'th token in the input.

If i is one past the end, then scan for another token.

Args:

restrict : [token, ...] or None; if restrict is None, then any
token is allowed.  You may call token(i) more than once.
However, the restrict set may never be larger than what was
passed in on the first call to token(i).

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Scanner.__repr__
documentation: Print the last 10 tokens that have been scanned in

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Scanner.interp_scan
documentation:
Should scan another token and add it to the list, self.tokens,
and add the restriction to self.restrictions

Yapps scanner.

The Yapps scanner can work in context sensitive or context
insensitive modes.  The token(i) method is used to retrieve the
i-th token.  It takes a restrict set that limits the set of tokens
it is allowed to return.  In context sensitive mode, this restrict
set guides the scanner.  In context insensitive mode, there is no
restriction (the set is always the full set of tokens).

classes.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Parser

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Parser._peek
documentation:
Returns the token type for lookahead; if there are any args
then the list of args is the set of token types to allow

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Parser._scan
documentation: Returns the matched text, and moves to the next token
Base class for Yapps-generated parsers.

classes.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Context

methods.name: yapps3_compiled_rt.Context.__init__
documentation:
Create a new context.

Args:
parent: Context object or None
scanner: Scanner object
pos: integer (scanner token position)
rule: string (name of the rule)
args: tuple listing parameters to the rule

Class to represent the parser's call stack.

Every rule creates a Context that links to its parent rule.  The
contexts can be used for debugging.

Unfortunately we cannot check loop item contents against non-loop contents
in a non-messy way during parsing, as we may not have easy access to previous
key value pairs in the context of our call (unlike our built-in access to all
previous loops).
For this reason, we don't waste time checking looped items against non-looped
names during parsing of a data block.  This would only match a subset of the

Note the following situations:

modules.name: drel
documentation: Dummy file

modules.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc
documentation: A dREL grammar written for python-ply
The output is an Abstract Syntax Tree that represents a
function fragment that needs to be wrapped with information
appropriate to the target language.
The grammar is based on the Python 2.7 grammar, in
consultation with Doug du Boulay's JsCifBrowser
grammar (also derived from a Python grammar).
To maximize python3/python2 compatibility

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_input
documentation:
input : maybe_nline statement
| input statement
Overall translation unit
Our input is a sequence of statements

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_statement
documentation:
statement : simple_stmt newlines
| simple_stmt ";" newlines
| compound_stmt
We distinguish between compound statements and
small statements. Small statements may be
chained together on a single line with semicolon
separators. Compound statements are not separated
in this way, and will always be terminated by
a newline.

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_simple_stmt
documentation:
simple_stmt : small_stmt
| simple_stmt ";" small_stmt
A simple statement is a sequence of small statements terminated by
a NEWLINE or EOF

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_statements
documentation:
statements : statement
| statements statement
This production appears inside a set of braces. Any statement
will be automatically terminated by a newline so we do not
need to include that here

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_small_stmt
documentation:
small_stmt :   expr_stmt
| print_stmt
| break_stmt
| next_stmt

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_break_stmt
documentation: break_stmt : BREAK

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_next_stmt
documentation: next_stmt : NEXT

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_print_stmt
documentation: print_stmt : PRINT expression

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_expr_stmt
documentation:
expr_stmt : testlist_star_expr
| testlist_star_expr AUGOP testlist_star_expr
| testlist_star_expr "=" testlist_star_expr
| fancy_drel_assignment_stmt
Note here that a simple testlist_star_expr is useless as in our
side-effect-free world it will be evaluated and discarded. We
could just drop it right now but we let it go through to the
AST processor for language-dependent processing

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_testlist_star_expr
documentation:
testlist_star_expr : expression
| testlist_star_expr "," maybe_nline expression

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_expression
documentation: expression : or_test
Simplified from the python 2.5 version due to apparent conflict with
the other type of IF expression...

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_or_test
documentation:
or_test : and_test
| or_test OR and_test
| or_test BADOR and_test
This is too generous, as it allows a function call on the
LHS to be assigned to.  This will cause a syntax error on
execution.

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_and_test
documentation:
and_test : not_test
| and_test AND not_test
| and_test BADAND not_test

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_not_test
documentation:
not_test : comparison
| NOT not_test

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_comparison
documentation:
comparison : a_expr
| a_expr comp_operator a_expr

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_comp_operator
documentation:
comp_operator : restricted_comp_operator
| IN
| NOT IN

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_restricted_comp_operator
documentation:
restricted_comp_operator :  "<"
| ">"
| GTE
| LTE
| NEQ
| ISEQUAL

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_a_expr
documentation:
a_expr : m_expr
| a_expr "+" m_expr
| a_expr "-" m_expr

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_m_expr
documentation:
m_expr : u_expr
| m_expr "*" u_expr
| m_expr "/" u_expr
| m_expr "^" u_expr
| m_expr "&" u_expr
| m_expr "|" u_expr

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_u_expr
documentation:
u_expr : power
| "-" u_expr
| "+" u_expr

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_power
documentation:
power : primary
| primary POWER u_expr

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_primary
documentation:
primary : atom
| attributeref
| subscription
| slicing
| call

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_atom
documentation:
atom : ID
| item_tag
| literal
| enclosure

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_item_tag
documentation: item_tag : ITEM_TAG

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_literal
documentation:
literal : stringliteral
| INTEGER
| HEXINT
| OCTINT
| BININT
| REAL
| IMAGINARY

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_stringliteral
documentation:
stringliteral : STRPREFIX SHORTSTRING
| STRPREFIX LONGSTRING
| SHORTSTRING
| LONGSTRING

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_enclosure
documentation:
enclosure : parenth_form
| string_conversion
| list_display
| table_display

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_parenth_form
documentation:
parenth_form : OPEN_PAREN testlist_star_expr CLOSE_PAREN
| OPEN_PAREN CLOSE_PAREN

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_string_conversion
documentation: string_conversion : "`" testlist_star_expr "`"

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_list_display
documentation:
list_display : "[" maybe_nline listmaker maybe_nline "]"
| "[" maybe_nline "]"

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_listmaker
documentation: listmaker : expression listmaker2
scrap the trailing comma

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_listmaker2
documentation:
listmaker2 : "," maybe_nline expression
| listmaker2 "," maybe_nline expression
|

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_table_display
documentation:
table_display : "{" maybe_nline tablemaker maybe_nline "}"
| "{" maybe_nline "}"
define tables

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_tablemaker
documentation: tablemaker :  stringliteral ":" expression tablemaker2

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_tablemaker2
documentation:
tablemaker2 : "," maybe_nline stringliteral ":" expression
| tablemaker2 "," maybe_nline stringliteral ":" expression
|

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_attributeref
documentation: attributeref : primary attribute_tag
Note that we need to catch tags of the form 't.12', which
our lexer will interpret as ID REAL.  We therefore also
accept t.12(3), which is not allowed, but we don't bother
trying to catch this error here.

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_attribute_tag
documentation:
attribute_tag : "." ID
| REAL

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_subscription
documentation: subscription : primary "[" expression "]"

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_slicing
documentation:
slicing :  primary "[" proper_slice "]"
|  primary "[" slice_list "]"

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_proper_slice
documentation:
proper_slice : short_slice
| long_slice

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_short_slice
documentation:
short_slice : ":"
| expression ":" expression
| ":" expression
| expression ":"
Our AST slice convention is that, if anything is mentioned,
the first element is always
explicitly mentioned. A single element will be a starting
element. Two elements are start and finish. An empty list
is all elements. Three elements are start, finish, step.
We combine these into a list of slices.

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_long_slice
documentation: long_slice : short_slice ":" expression

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_slice_list
documentation:
slice_list : slice_item
| slice_list "," slice_item

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_slice_item
documentation:
slice_item : expression
| proper_slice

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_call
documentation:
call : ID OPEN_PAREN CLOSE_PAREN
| ID OPEN_PAREN argument_list CLOSE_PAREN

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_argument_list
documentation:
argument_list : func_arg
| argument_list "," func_arg
These are the arguments to a call, not a definition

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_func_arg
documentation: func_arg : expression

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_fancy_drel_assignment_stmt
documentation: fancy_drel_assignment_stmt : ID OPEN_PAREN dotlist CLOSE_PAREN

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_dotlist
documentation:
dotlist : "." ID "=" expression
| dotlist "," "." ID "=" expression
print("Fancy assignment -> {!r}".format(p[0]))
Something made up specially for drel.  A newline is OK between assignments

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_exprlist
documentation:
exprlist : a_expr
| exprlist "," a_expr

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_id_list
documentation:
id_list : ID
| id_list "," ID
a (potentially enclosed) list of ids

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_compound_stmt
documentation:
compound_stmt : if_stmt
| if_else_stmt
| for_stmt
| do_stmt
| loop_stmt
| with_stmt
| repeat_stmt
| funcdef
now for the compound statements. We prepare them as a "STATEMENT"
list for smooth processing in the simple_stmt production

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_if_else_stmt
documentation: if_else_stmt : if_stmt ELSE suite
There must only be one else statement at the end of the else if statements,
so we show this by creating a separate production

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_if_stmt
documentation:
if_stmt : IF OPEN_PAREN expression CLOSE_PAREN maybe_nline suite
| if_stmt ELSEIF OPEN_PAREN expression CLOSE_PAREN maybe_nline suite
The AST node is [IF_EXPR,cond, suite,[[elseif cond1,suite],[elseifcond2,suite]...]]

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_suite
documentation:
suite : statement
| "{" maybe_nline statements "}" maybe_nline
Note the dREL divergence from Python here: we allow compound
statements to follow without a separate block (like C etc.)  Where
we have a single statement immediately following we have to make the
statement block.  A small_stmt will be a single production, so must
be put into a list in order to match the 'statements' structure
(i.e. 2nd element is a list of statements).  A compound_stmt is thus
forced to be also a non-listed object.

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_for_stmt
documentation:
for_stmt : FOR id_list IN testlist_star_expr suite
| FOR "[" id_list "]" IN testlist_star_expr suite

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_loop_stmt
documentation: loop_stmt : loop_head suite

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_loop_head
documentation:
loop_head : LOOP ID AS ID
| LOOP ID AS ID ":" ID
| LOOP ID AS ID ":" ID restricted_comp_operator ID
We capture a list of all the actually present items in the current
datafile

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_do_stmt
documentation: do_stmt : do_stmt_head suite

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_do_stmt_head
documentation:
do_stmt_head : DO ID "=" expression "," expression
| DO ID "=" expression "," expression "," expression
To translate the dREL do to a for statement, we need to make the
end of the range included in the range

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_repeat_stmt
documentation: repeat_stmt : REPEAT suite

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_with_stmt
documentation: with_stmt : with_head maybe_nline suite

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_with_head
documentation: with_head : WITH ID AS ID

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_funcdef
documentation: funcdef : FUNCTION ID OPEN_PAREN arglist CLOSE_PAREN suite

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_arglist
documentation:
arglist : ID ":" list_display
| arglist "," ID ":" list_display

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_maybe_nline
documentation:
maybe_nline : newlines
| empty
This production allows us to insert optional newlines

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_newlines
documentation:
newlines : NEWLINE
| newlines NEWLINE
We need to allow multiple newlines here and not just in the lexer as
an intervening comment can cause multiple newline tokens to appear

functions.name: drel.drel_ast_yacc.p_empty
documentation: empty       :

modules.name: StarFile
documentation: To maximize python3/python2 compatibility

functions.name: StarFile.ReadStar
documentation:
Read in a STAR file, returning the contents in the `prepared` object.

* `filename` may be a URL, a file
path on the local system, or any object with a `read` method.

* `prepared` provides a `StarFile` or `CifFile` object that the contents of `filename`
will be added to.

* `maxlength` is the maximum allowable line length in the input file. This has been set at
2048 characters for CIF but is unlimited (-1) for STAR files.

* `grammar` chooses the STAR grammar variant. `1.0` is the original 1992 CIF/STAR grammar and `1.1`
is identical except for the exclusion of square brackets as the first characters in
undelimited datanames. `2.0` will read files in the CIF2.0 standard, and `STAR2` will
read files according to the STAR2 publication.  If grammar is `None` or `auto`, autodetection
will be attempted in the order `2.0`, `1.1` and `1.0`. This will always succeed for conformant CIF2.0 files.
Note that (nested) save frames are read in all grammar variations and then flagged afterwards if
they do not match the requested grammar.

* `scantype` can be `standard` or `flex`.  `standard` provides pure Python parsing at the
cost of a factor of 10 or so in speed.  `flex` will tokenise the input CIF file using
fast C routines.  Note that running PyCIFRW in Jython uses native Java regular expressions
to provide a speedup regardless of this argument.

* `CBF` flags that the input file is in Crystallographic Binary File format. The binary block is
excised from the input data stream before parsing and is not available in the returned object.

* `permissive` allows non UTF8 encodings (currently only latin1) in the input file. These are a 
violation of the standard.

functions.name: StarFile.apply_line_folding
documentation: Insert line folding characters into instring between min/max wraplength

functions.name: StarFile.remove_line_folding
documentation: Remove line folding from instring

functions.name: StarFile.apply_line_prefix
documentation: Prefix every line in instring with prefix

functions.name: StarFile.remove_line_prefix
documentation: Remove prefix from every line if present

functions.name: StarFile.transpose
documentation: Transpose the list of lists passed to us

functions.name: StarFile.not_none
documentation: Return true only if no values of None are present
This routine optimised to return as quickly as possible
as it is called a lot.

functions.name: StarFile.check_stringiness
documentation: Check that the contents of data are all strings

functions.name: StarFile.process_template
documentation: Process a template datafile to formatting instructions

methods.name: StarFile.LoopBlock.RemoveItem
documentation: Remove `itemname` from the block.

methods.name: StarFile.LoopBlock.RemoveLoopItem

documentation:
Return a `StarFile.LoopBlock` object constructed from the loop containing `keyname`.
`keyname` is only significant as a way to specify the loop.

methods.name: StarFile.LoopBlock.GetItemOrder
documentation:
Return a list of datanames in this `LoopBlock` in the order that they will be
printed

methods.name: StarFile.LoopBlock.ChangeItemOrder
documentation: Change the position at which `itemname` appears when printing out to `newpos`.

methods.name: StarFile.LoopBlock.GetItemPosition
documentation:
A utility function to get the numerical order in the printout
of `itemname`.  An item has coordinate `(loop_no,pos)` with
the top level having a `loop_no` of -1.  If an integer is passed to
the routine then it will return the position of the loop
referenced by that number.

methods.name: StarFile.LoopBlock.GetLoopNames
documentation: Return all datanames appearing together with `keyname`

methods.name: StarFile.LoopBlock.AddToLoop
documentation:
*Deprecated*. Use `AddItem` followed by calls to `AddLoopName`.

Add multiple columns to the loop containing `dataname`. `loopdata` is a
collection of (key,value) pairs, where `key` is the new dataname and `value`
is a list of values for that dataname

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.setmaxnamelength
documentation: Set the maximum allowable dataname length (-1 for no check)

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.set_characterset
documentation: Set the characterset for checking datanames: may be `ascii` or `unicode`

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.keys
documentation: keys returns all internal keys

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.has_key_or_alias
documentation: Check if a dataname or alias is available in the block

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.copy
documentation: doesn't appear to work

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.GetItemPosition
documentation:
A utility function to get the numerical order in the printout
of `itemname`.  An item has coordinate `(loop_no,pos)` with
the top level having a `loop_no` of -1.  If an integer is passed to
the routine then it will return the position of the loop
referenced by that number.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.ChangeItemOrder
documentation:
Move the printout order of `itemname` to `newpos`. If `itemname` is
in a loop, `newpos` refers to the order within the loop.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.GetItemOrder
documentation:
Return a list of datanames in the order in which they will be printed.  Loops are
referred to by numerical index

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.AddItem
documentation:
Add dataname `key` to block with value `value`.  `value` may be
a single value, a list or a tuple. If `precheck` is False (the default),
all values will be checked and converted to unicode strings as necessary. If
`precheck` is True, this checking is bypassed.  No checking is necessary
when values are read from a CIF file as they are already in correct form.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.AddLoopItem
documentation:
*Deprecated*. Use `AddItem` followed by `CreateLoop` if
necessary.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.regularise_data
documentation: Place dataitem into a list if necessary

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.RemoveItem
documentation: Remove `itemname` from the block.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.RemoveLoopItem
documentation: *Deprecated*. Use `RemoveItem` instead

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.GetItemValue
documentation:
Return value of `itemname`.  If `itemname` is looped, a list
of all values will be returned.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.GetFullItemValue
documentation:
Return the value associated with `itemname`, and a boolean flagging whether
(True) or not (False) it is in a form suitable for calculation.  False is
always returned for strings and `StarList` objects.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.CreateLoop
documentation:
Create a loop in the datablock. `datanames` is a list of datanames that
together form a loop.  If length_check is True, they should have been initialised in the block
to have the same number of elements (possibly 0). If `order` is given,
the loop will appear at this position in the block when printing
out. A single-row loop will be created if the provided datanames are all
non-lists. A loop counts as a single position.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.AddLoopName
documentation:
Add `newname` to the loop containing `oldname`. If it is already in the new loop, no
error is raised.  If `newname` is in a different loop, it is removed from that loop.
The number of values associated with `newname` must match the number of values associated
with all other columns of the new loop or a `ValueError` will be raised.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.FindLoop
documentation:
Find the loop that contains `keyname` and return its numerical index or
-1 if not present. The numerical index can be used to refer to the loop in
other routines.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.GetLoop
documentation:
Return a `StarFile.LoopBlock` object constructed from the loop containing `keyname`.
`keyname` is only significant as a way to specify the loop.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.GetLoopNames
documentation: Return all datanames appearing together with `keyname`

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.AddLoopName
documentation:
Add `newname` to the loop containing `oldname`. If it is already in the new loop, no
error is raised.  If `newname` is in a different loop, it is removed from that loop.
The number of values associated with `newname` must match the number of values associated
with all other columns of the new loop or a `ValueError` will be raised.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.AddToLoop
documentation:
*Deprecated*. Use `AddItem` followed by calls to `AddLoopName`.

Add multiple columns to the loop containing `dataname`. `loopdata` is a
collection of (key,value) pairs, where `key` is the new dataname and `value`
is a list of values for that dataname

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.RemoveKeyedPacket
documentation:
Remove the packet for which dataname `keyname` takes
value `keyvalue`.  Only the first such occurrence is
removed.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.GetKeyedPacket
documentation:
Return the loop packet (a `StarPacket` object) where `keyname` has value
`keyvalue`. Ignore case in `keyvalue` if `no_case` is True.  `ValueError`
is raised if no packet is found or more than one packet is found.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.GetCompoundKeyedPacket
documentation:
Return the loop packet (a `StarPacket` object) where the `{key:(value,caseless)}` pairs
in `keydict` take the appropriate values. Ignore case for a given `key` if `caseless` is
True.  `ValueError` is raised if no packet is found or more than one packet is found.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.GetKeyedSemanticPacket
documentation:
Return a complete packet for category `cat_id` where the
category key for the category equals `keyvalue`.  This routine
will understand any joined loops, so if separate loops in the
datafile belong to the
same category hierarchy (e.g. `_atom_site` and `_atom_site_aniso`),
the returned `StarPacket` object will contain datanames from
both categories.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.GetMultiKeyedSemanticPacket
documentation:
Return a complete packet for category `cat_id` where the keyvalues are
provided as a dictionary of key:(value,caseless) pairs
This routine
will understand any joined loops, so if separate loops in the
datafile belong to the
same category hierarchy (e.g. `_atom_site` and `_atom_site_aniso`),
the returned `StarPacket` object will contain datanames from
the requested category and any children.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.SetOutputLength
documentation:
Set the maximum output line length (`maxoutlength`) and the line length to
wrap at (`wraplength`).  The wrap length is a target only and may not always be
possible.

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.format_names
documentation: Print datanames from `loop_no` one per line

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.format_value

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.create_ordering

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.do_wrapping
documentation: Wrap the provided string

methods.name: StarFile.StarBlock.unassign_dictionary
documentation: Remove dictionary-dependent behaviour

methods.name: StarFile.StarPacket.merge_packet
documentation: Merge contents of incoming packet with this packet

documentation: Derive a missing attribute

Set the allowed characters for datanames and datablocks: may be `ascii` or `unicode`. If datanames
have already been added to any datablocks, they are not checked.

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.unlock
documentation: Allow overwriting of all blocks in this collection

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.lock
documentation: Disallow overwriting for all blocks in this collection

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.__delitem__
documentation: we have to get an ordered list of the current keys,
as we'll have to delete one of them anyway.
Deletion will delete any key regardless of visibility

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.__contains__
documentation: Support the 'in' operator

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.__iter__
documentation: We iterate over all visible

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.keys
documentation: TODO: handle different case

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.has_key
documentation: Note that has_key does not exist in 3.5

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.first_block
documentation: Return the 'first' block.  This is not necessarily the first block in the file.

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.NewBlock
documentation:
Add a new block named `blockname` with contents `blockcontents`. If `fix`
is True, `blockname` will have spaces and tabs replaced by underscores. `parent`
allows a parent block to be set so that block hierarchies can be created.  Depending on
the output standard, these blocks will be printed out as nested save frames or
ignored.

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection._rekey
documentation:
The block with key [[oldname]] gets [[newname]] as a new key, but the printed name
does not change unless [[block_id]] is given.  Prefer [[rename]] for a safe version.

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.rename
documentation:
Rename datablock from [[oldname]] to [[newname]]. Both key and printed name are changed.  No
conformance checks are conducted.

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.makebc
documentation: Make a block collection from a list of block names

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.merge_fast
documentation:
Do a fast merge. WARNING: this may change one or more of its frame headers in order to
remove duplicate frames.  Please keep a handle to the block object instead of the text of
the header.

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.get_parent
documentation: Return the name of the block enclosing [[blockname]] in canonical form (lower case)

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.get_roots
documentation: Get the top-level blocks

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.get_children
documentation:
Get all children of [[blockname]] as a block collection. If [[include_parent]] is
True, the parent block will also be included in the block collection as the root.

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.get_immediate_children
documentation: Get the next level of children of the given block as a list, without nested levels

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.get_child_list
documentation: Get a list of all child categories in alphabetical order
This takes time

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.is_child_of_parent
documentation: Return `True` if `blockname` is a child of `parentname`

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.set_parent
documentation: Set the parent block

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.SetTemplate
documentation: Use `template_file` as a template for all block output

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.WriteOut
documentation:
Return the contents of this file as a string, wrapping if possible at `wraplength`
characters and restricting maximum line length to `maxoutlength`.  Delimiters and
save frame nesting are controlled by `self.grammar`. If `blockorder` is
provided, blocks are output in this order unless nested save frames have been
requested (STAR2). The default block order is the order in which blocks were input.
`saves_after` inserts all save frames after the given dataname,
which allows less important items to appear later.  Useful in conjunction with a
template for dictionary files.

methods.name: StarFile.BlockCollection.block_to_string_nested
documentation:
Output a complete datablock indexed by [[block_ref]] and named [[block_id]], including children,
and syntactically nesting save frames

A container for StarBlock objects. The constructor takes
one non-keyword argument `datasource` to set the initial data.  If
`datasource` is a Python dictionary, the values must be `StarBlock`
objects and the keys will be blocknames in the new object. Keyword
arguments:

standard:
    `CIF` or `Dic`.  `CIF` enforces 75-character blocknames, and will
    print block contents before that block's save frame.

blocktype:
    The type of blocks held in this container. Normally `StarBlock`
    or `CifBlock`.

characterset:
    `ascii` or `unicode`.  Blocknames and datanames appearing within
    blocks are restricted to the appropriate characterset. Note that
    only characters in the basic multilingual plane are accepted. This
    restriction will be lifted when PyCIFRW is ported to Python3.

scoping:
    `instance` or `dictionary`: `instance` implies that save frames are
    hidden from save frames lower in the hierarchy or in sibling
    hierarchies. `dictionary` makes all save frames visible everywhere
    within a data block.  This setting is only relevant for STAR2 dictionaries and
    STAR2 data files, as save frames are currently not used in plain CIF data
    files.

methods.name: StarFile.CIFStringIO.write
documentation: Write a string with correct linebreak, tabs and indents

methods.name: StarFile.CIFStringIO.set_tab
documentation: Set the tab stop position

modules.name: drel.drel_lex

functions.name: drel.drel_lex.t_INITIAL_OPEN_PAREN
documentation: We do not have this as a literal so that we can switch to ignoring newlines

functions.name: drel.drel_lex.t_IMAGINARY
documentation: Do the reals before the integers, otherwise the integer will
match the first part of the real

functions.name: drel.drel_lex.t_BININT
documentation: Do the binary,octal etc before decimal integer otherwise the 0 at
the front will match the decimal integer 0

functions.name: drel.drel_lex.t_LONGSTRING
documentation: try longstring first as otherwise the '' will match a shortstring

functions.name: drel.drel_lex.t_ELSEIF
documentation: special to avoid any ambiguity

functions.name: drel.drel_lex.t_ITEM_TAG
documentation: Item tags can have underscores and digits inside, and must have
at least one underscore at the front

variables.name: drel.drel_lex.literals
documentation: '&' and '|' are pycifrw extensions

modules.name: YappsStarParser_2_0
documentation: To maximize python3/python2 compatibility

functions.name: YappsStarParser_2_0.stripextras
documentation: Strip extras gets rid of leading and trailing whitespace, and
semicolons.

functions.name: YappsStarParser_2_0.stripstring
documentation: helper function to get rid of inverted commas etc.

functions.name: YappsStarParser_2_0.striptriple
documentation: helper function to get rid of triple quotes

functions.name: YappsStarParser_2_0.makeloop
documentation: helper function to populate a StarBlock given a list of names
and values .
Note that there may be an empty list at the very end of our itemlists,
so we remove that if necessary.

functions.name: YappsStarParser_2_0.make_empty
documentation: return an object with the appropriate amount of nesting

functions.name: YappsStarParser_2_0.cif_update
documentation: this function updates a dictionary first checking for name collisions,
which imply that the CIF is invalid.  We need case insensitivity for
names.
Unfortunately we cannot check loop item contents against non-loop contents
in a non-messy way during parsing, as we may not have easy access to previous
key value pairs in the context of our call (unlike our built-in access to all
previous loops).
For this reason, we don't waste time checking looped items against non-looped
names during parsing of a data block.  This would only match a subset of the
final items.   We do check against ordinary items, however.
Note the following situations:
(1) new_dict is empty -> we have just added a loop; do no checking
(2) new_dict is not empty -> we have some new key-value pairs

functions.name: YappsStarParser_2_0.order_update
documentation: this takes two lines, so we couldn't fit it into a one line execution statement...

functions.name: YappsStarParser_2_0.pairwise
documentation: and finally...turn a sequence into a python dict (thanks to Stackoverflow)

names.
Unfortunately we cannot check loop item contents against non-loop contents
in a non-messy way during parsing, as we may not have easy access to previous
key value pairs in the context of our call (unlike our built-in access to all

For this reason, we don't waste time checking looped items against non-looped
names during parsing of a data block.  This would only match a subset of the
final items.   We do check against ordinary items, however.
Note the following situations:
(1) new_dict is empty -> we have just added a loop; do no checking
(2) new_dict is not empty -> we have some new key-value pairs

functions.name: CifFile_module.Validate
documentation:
Validate the `ciffile` conforms to the definitions in `CifDic` object `dic`, or if `dic` is missing,
to the results of merging the `CifDic` objects in `diclist` according to `mergemode`.  Flag
`isdic` indicates that `ciffile` is a CIF dictionary meaning that save frames should be
accessed for validation and that mandatory_category should be interpreted differently for DDL2.

functions.name: CifFile_module.error_report
documentation: A function to lay out a single error report.  We are passed
the name of the error (one of our validation functions), the
explanation to print out, and a dictionary with the error
information.  We print no more than 50 characters of the item

functions.name: CifFile_module.ReadCif
documentation:
Read in a CIF file, returning a `CifFile` object.

* `filename` may be a URL, a file
path on the local system, or any object with a `read` method.

* `grammar` chooses the CIF grammar variant. `1.0` is the original 1992 grammar and `1.1`
is identical except for the exclusion of square brackets as the first characters in
undelimited datanames. `2.0` will read files in the CIF2.0 standard, and `STAR2` will
read files according to the STAR2 publication.  If grammar is `None`, autodetection
will be attempted in the order `2.0`, `1.1` and `1.0`. This will always succeed for
properly-formed CIF2.0 files.  Note that only Unicode characters in the basic multilingual
plane are recognised (this will be fixed when PyCIFRW is ported to Python 3).

* `scantype` can be `standard` or `flex`.  `standard` provides pure Python parsing at the
cost of a factor of 10 or so in speed.  `flex` will tokenise the input CIF file using
fast C routines, but is not available for CIF2/STAR2 files.  Note that running PyCIFRW in
Jython uses native Java regular expressions
to provide a speedup regardless of this argument (and does not yet support CIF2).

* `scoping` is only relevant where nested save frames are expected (STAR2 only).
`instance` scoping makes nested save frames
invisible outside their hierarchy, allowing duplicate save frame names in separate
hierarchies. `dictionary` scoping makes all save frames within a data block visible to each
other, thereby restricting all save frames to have unique names.
Currently the only recognised value for `standard` is `CIF`, which when set enforces a
maximum length of 75 characters for datanames and has no other effect.

classes.name: CifFile_module.CifBlock

methods.name: CifFile_module.CifBlock.__init__
documentation:
When provided, `data` should be another CifBlock whose contents will be copied to
this block.

* if `strict` is set, maximum name lengths will be enforced

* `maxoutlength` is the maximum length for output lines

* `wraplength` is the ideal length to make output lines

* When set, `overwrite` allows the values of datanames to be changed (otherwise an error
is raised).

* `compat_mode` will allow deprecated behaviour of creating single-dataname loops using
the syntax `a[_dataname] = [1,2,3,4]`.  This should now be done by calling `CreateLoop`
after setting the dataitem value.

methods.name: CifFile_module.CifBlock.RemoveCifItem
documentation: Remove `itemname` from the CifBlock

methods.name: CifFile_module.CifBlock.AddCifItem
documentation: *DEPRECATED*. Use `AddItem` instead.

methods.name: CifFile_module.CifBlock.AddSingleCifItem
documentation: *Deprecated*. Use `AddItem` instead

A class to hold a single block of a CIF file.  A `CifBlock` object can be treated as
a Python dictionary, in particular, individual items can be accessed using square
brackets e.g. `b['_a_dataname']`.  All other Python dictionary methods are also
available (e.g. `keys()`, `values()`).  Looped datanames will return a list of values.

## Initialisation

When provided, `data` should be another `CifBlock` whose contents will be copied to
this block.

* if `strict` is set, maximum name lengths will be enforced

* `maxoutlength` is the maximum length for output lines

* `wraplength` is the ideal length to make output lines

* When set, `overwrite` allows the values of datanames to be changed (otherwise an error
is raised).

* `compat_mode` will allow deprecated behaviour of creating single-dataname loops using
the syntax `a[_dataname] = [1,2,3,4]`.  This should now be done by calling `CreateLoop`
after setting the dataitem value.

methods.name: CifFile_module.CifDic.add_category
documentation:
Add a new category to the dictionary with name [[catname]].
If [[catparent]] is None, the category will be a child of
the topmost 'Head' category or else the top data block. If
[[is_loop]] is false, a Set category is created. If [[allow_dangler]]
is true, the parent category does not have to exist.
Note that our semantic parent is given by catparent, but our syntactic parent is
always just the root block

methods.name: CifFile_module.CifDic.add_definition
documentation:
Add itemname to category [[catparent]]. If itemname contains periods,
all text before the final period is ignored. If [[allow_dangler]] is True,
no check for a parent category is made.

documentation: Return an error if any mandatory items are missing

methods.name: CifFile_module.CifDic.check_prohibited_items
documentation: Return an error if any prohibited items are present

Create a Cif Dictionary object from the provided source, which can
be a filename/URL or a CifFile.  Optional arguments (relevant to DDLm
only):

* do_minimum (Boolean):
     Do not set up the dREL system for auto-calculation or perform
     imports.  This implies do_imports=False and do_dREL=False

* do_imports = No/Full/Contents/All:
     If not 'No', intepret _import.get statements for
     Full mode/Contents mode/Both respectively. See also option 'heavy'

* do_dREL = True/False:
     Parse and convert all dREL methods to Python. Implies do_imports=All

* heavy = True/False:
     (Experimental). If True, importation overwrites definitions. If False,
     attributes are resolved dynamically.

classes.name: CifFile_module.ValidCifBlock

methods.name: CifFile_module.ValidCifBlock.AddItem
documentation: Set value of dataname `key` to `value` after checking for conformance with CIF dictionary

methods.name: CifFile_module.ValidCifBlock.report_if_invalid
documentation: utility function

A `CifBlock` that is valid with respect to a given CIF dictionary.  Methods
of `CifBlock` are overridden where necessary to disallow addition of invalid items to the
`CifBlock`.

## Initialisation

* `dic` is a `CifDic` object to be used for validation.

initialisation specifies a `CifDic` object to use for validation.
